context: Tests whether the solver keeps swift-language focused on core Swift type-system semantics and routes neighboring Codable, FormatStyle/localization, and API naming concerns to sibling skills.

checklist.name: Language owner
checklist.description: Says swift-language owns the core some/any/Never type-system decision.

checklist.name: Some guidance
checklist.description: Uses some for generic parameters or opaque returns when one concrete type is preserved by the compiler.

checklist.name: Any guidance
checklist.description: Uses any for heterogeneous storage, dynamic type erasure, or runtime flexibility.

checklist.name: Never caveat
checklist.description: Explains that Never is uninhabited or bottom-like only in appropriate expression/generic contexts and does not implicitly conform to arbitrary protocols.

checklist.name: Codable route
checklist.description: Routes full API response models, snake_case key strategy, and date decoding to swift-codable.

checklist.name: Format route
checklist.description: Routes detailed market-sensitive formatting to swift-formatstyle and mentions ios-localization for market, copy, or localized-display review.

checklist.name: Naming route
checklist.description: Routes public helper names, labels, and documentation-comment review to swift-api-design-guidelines when that work appears.

checklist.name: No sibling implementation
checklist.description: May include concise sibling-owner examples or rules, but does not provide an end-to-end decoding pipeline, formatting matrix, localization plan, or complete API naming rewrite.
